items.title: Great White Sharks Have Been in the Mediterranean Sea for Millions of Years—but Sightings Are Incredibly Rare
items.summary: A recent video of a great white shark in the Mediterranean Sea offers the possibility of deriving valuable information for conservation strategies.

items.title: GM Wants Your Electric Car to Power Your House—and Your Neighborhood
items.summary: The automaker today is turning on vehicle-to-grid charging for its GM Energy customers. Will people actually use it?

items.title: MacOS 27 Golden Gate: Top New Features
items.summary: Apple has announced the latest version of macOS. It’s all about the reintroduction of Siri, which is now accessible from anywhere on the Mac desktop.

items.title: GM joins race to build batteries for AI data centers and the grid
items.summary: GM is developing an entirely new sodium-ion battery chemistry for use in everything from data centers to its own factories.

items.title: Hey, Siri, here’s what I actually want from AI
items.summary: I'm desperate for a personal AI assistant, but do I really want to become the kind of person who can't function without the friendly robot voice in my phone?

items.summary: Anthropic's Claude Fable 5 is going to be a big hit with the web's vibe coders.

items.title: Inside Myanmar, rebels are losing ground as military forces men into army
items.summary: The BBC travels with rebels to frontline positions in Myanmar to see how the war is unfolding.

items.title: US striking Iran in response to downing of helicopter, military says
items.summary: President Donald Trump earlier accused Iran of shooting down the US helicopter over the Strait of Hormuz and vowed to respond.

items.title: Israeli air strikes hit Lebanese city of Tyre despite Iranian warning to stop attacks
items.summary: Iran warned Israel on Monday that it could resume hostilities if attacks on its Lebanese ally Hezbollah do not stop.

items.title: Kalshi trading in 'perps' crosses $1 billion in volume within a week of launch
items.summary: A spokesperson for the company said that the product is the fastest growing in the prediction market platform's history.

items.title: JPMorgan Chase plans to deploy more powerful AI agents this year
items.summary: JPMorgan Chase's move suggests long-running AI agents are close to clearing the security and governance hurdles that have slowed adoption inside big companies.

items.title: Electric vehicle giant BYD predicts 80% of China car sales will soon be electric
items.summary: BYD expects China's competitive electric vehicle market to continue growing, even as manufacturers and analysts anticipate domestic demand to taper further.

items.title: Stocks Sink in Broad AI Rout Sparked by China's DeepSeek
items.summary: U.S. stocks were mostly lower, with the Nasdaq leading declines as makers of AI infrastructure suffered steep falls, many in the double digits. Nvidia was down 16%.

items.title: Comex Gold, Silver Settle Lower
items.summary: Gold settled 1.4% lower, down two of the past three sessions, and silver fell 2.5%, down three of the past four sessions.

items.title: DeepSeek Won't Sink U.S. AI Titans
items.summary: Panic fueling the selloff of Nvidia, Broadcom and other tech giants is overblown.

items.title: Meta accuses Israeli spyware firm of again targeting WhatsApp users
items.summary: Meta accused an Israeli spyware firm on Monday of violating a court order restricting phishing attempts on WhatsApp, a social messaging platform owned by the technology company.   In an online statement, Meta announced it has asked a federal court to hold NSO Group in contempt for alleged ties to hacking attempts targeting WhatsApp users. The…

items.title: DOJ finds EEOC guidelines on workplace discrimination unconstitutional
items.summary: The Justice Department on Tuesday found the Equal Employment Opportunity Commission’s (EEOC) hiring guidelines unconstitutional, finding they pressure employers to take race into consideration. The opinion from the Justice Department’s Office of Legal Counsel (OLC) is not a court opinion but could nonetheless make it more difficult for employees to bring discrimination claims against their…

items.title: Live updates: Consequential primaries in Maine, South Carolina; House passes reconciliation bill
items.summary: The Maine Senate race, roiled by controversy last week, will be formalized Tuesday, with primaries set to lock in progressive oyster farmer Graham Platner’s bid to unseat Sen. Susan Collins (R). Platner has faced a growing heap of controversies that threaten to derail his bid for a must-win seat in the Democratic fight for Senate control this…

items.title: Planet nine mystery deepens as new discovery challenges hidden planet theory
items.summary: Astronomers have spent years searching for a possible hidden giant planet far beyond Neptune. Unusual orbits among distant Kuiper Belt objects have fueled the Planet Nine theory, but recent discoveries are challenging the idea by showing more stable motion than expected. If Planet Nine exists, it may be much farther away than originally thought.

items.title: NASA updates worsening ISS leak after crew safety alert
items.summary: NASA says a long-running air leak aboard the ISS recently worsened, leading engineers to investigate new suspected crack locations and consider a riskier repair strategy. Astronauts were temporarily moved into a safe haven as a precaution before the repair was postponed for further analysis.

items.title: Scientists found a new Alzheimer’s trigger and a drug that stops it
items.summary: Researchers have identified a new Alzheimer’s target and created an experimental compound that blocks a damaging process inside brain cells. In mice, the treatment slowed nerve cell loss, reduced Alzheimer’s-related changes, and even appeared to promote healthier aging.

items.title: Frozen squirrel poop hints at sights and smells of Ice Age ecosystems
items.summary: DNA preserved in ancient scat reveals what Yukon ground squirrels ate and what animals shared their world.

items.title: These birds clack their wing bones together to woo mates at night
items.summary: During courtship, male scissor-tailed nightjars crack their wings together to make a sharp snapping sound. It's the result of colliding arm bones.

items.title: Some pterosaurs may have boasted bold iridescence
items.summary: A new analysis of a 120-million-year-old fossil suggests at least one pterosaur species shimmered in iridescent greens and magentas.
